description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: U.S. Navy federal service academy in the Annapolis, Maryland area
description_wiki: The United States Naval Academy (USNA, Navy, or Annapolis) is a federal service academy adjacent to Annapolis, Maryland.

description: city in and county seat of Anne Arundel County, Maryland, United States, and Maryland federated state capital city
description_wiki: Annapolis (  ə-NAP-əl-iss) is the capital of the U.S. state of Maryland.

description: terrestrial continent located in north-western Eurasia
description_wiki: Europe is a continent located entirely in the Northern Hemisphere and mostly in the Eastern Hemisphere.
World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

description: United States Armed Forces decoration award
description_wiki: The Bronze Star Medal (BSM) is a United States Armed Forces decoration awarded to members of the United States Armed Forces for either heroic achievement, heroic service, meritorious achievement, or meritorious service in a combat zone.

description: U.S. Army individual decoration for heroism, meritorious achievement, or meritorious service
seat of Pulaski County, and capital and largest city of Arkansas, United States
description_wiki: Little Rock is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Arkansas.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.
USS Cumberland (IX-8), an unclassified miscellaneous vessel, was the second ship of the United States Navy to be named for the Cumberland River.

description: town and civil parish in Devon, United Kingdom
description_wiki: Salcombe is a resort town in the South Hams district of Devon, in south west England.
South Devon is the southern part of Devon, England.
Italy, officially the Italian Republic, is a country in Southern and Western Europe.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: former hospital in Colorado, United States
description_wiki: Fitzsimons Army Hospital, also known as Fitzsimons General Hospital, renamed Fitzsimons Army Medical Center in 1974, and Fitzsimons Building in 2018 was a U.S. Army facility located on 577 acres (234 ha) in Aurora, Colorado.

description: former US Army post in Indianapolis, IN, US 1906-1991
description_wiki: Fort Benjamin Harrison was a U.S. Army post located in suburban Lawrence Township, Marion County, Indiana, northeast of Indianapolis, between 1906 and 1991.

aliases: Amateur Sports Capital of the World
description: capital city of the U.S. state of Indiana and the seat of Marion County
description_wiki: Indianapolis ( , IN-dee-ə-NAP-ə-lis), colloquially known as Indy, is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Indiana and the county seat of Marion County.
